title: 第 24 節
en: Ninth Khanda.

1. Let a man meditate on the sevenfold Saman as the sun. The sun is Saman, because he is always the same (sama) ; he is Saman because he is the same, everybody thinking he looks towards me, he looks towards me 2.

2. Let him know that all beings are dependent on him (the sun). What he is before his rising, that is the hiiikara. On it animals are dependent. Therefore animals say hin (before sunrise), for they share the hiiikara of that Saman (the sun).
3. What he is when first risen, that is the prastava. On it men are dependent. Therefore men love praise (prastuti) and celebrity, for they share the prastava of that Saman.
1 These are again the stobhaksharas, or musical syllables used in the performance of the Saman hymns; see p. 22.
2 Cf. Kh. Up. II, 2, 2. Comm. '

II PRAPA777AKA, 9 KHAAT£A, 8. 27 4. What he is at the time of the sarigava l, that is the adi, the first, the Om. On it birds are dependent. Therefore birds fly about in the sky without support, holding themselves, for they share the adi 2 (the Om) of that Saman.

5. What he is just at noon, that is the udgitha. On it the Devas are dependent (because they are brilliant). Therefore they are the best of all the descendants of Pra^apati, for they share the udgitha of that Saman.

6. What he is after midday and before afternoon, that is the pratihara. On it all germs are dependent. Therefore these, having been conceived (pratihrz'ta), do not fall, for they share the pratihara of that Saman.

7. What he is after the afternoon and before sunset, that is the upadrava. On it the animals of the forest are dependent. Therefore, when they see a man, they run (upadravanti) to the forest as a safe hiding-place, for they share the upadrava of that Saman.

8. What he is when he first sets, that is the nidhana. On it the fathers are dependent. Therefore they put them 3 down (nidadhati), for they share the nidhana of that Saman. Thus a man meditates on the sevenfold Saman as the sun.

1 When the sun puts forth his rays, and when the cows are together with their calves, i. e. as Rajendralal Mitra says, after the cows have been milked and are allowed by the cowherds to suckle their young.
2 The tertium comparationis is here the a. of adi and the a of adaya, i. e. holding. The d might have been added.
3 The cakes for the ancestral spirits, or the spirits themselves.
28 ##andogya-upanishad.

Tenth Kuanda. i. Next let a man meditate on the sevenfold Saman which is uniform in itself1 and leads beyond death. The word hiiikara has three syllables, the word prastava has three syllables : that is equal (sama).
2. The word adi (first, Om) has two syllables, the word pratihara has four syllables. Taking one syllable from that over, that is equal (sama).

3. The word udgitha has three syllables, the word upadrava has four syllables. With three and three syllables it should be equal. One syllable being left over, it becomes trisyllabic. Hence it is equal.
4. The word nidhana has three syllables, therefore it is equal. These make twenty-two syllables.

5. With twenty-one syllables a man reaches the sun (and death), for the sun is the twenty-first2 from here ; with the twenty-second he conquers what is beyond the sun : that is blessedness, that is freedom from grief.

6. He obtains here the victory over the sun (death), and there is a higher victory than the victory over the sun for him, who knowing this meditates on the sevenfold Saman as uniform in itself, which leads beyond death, yea, which leads beyond death.

Eleventh Kuanda 3. 1. The hiiikara is mind, the prastava speech, the udgitha sight, the pratihara hearing, the nidhana 1 Atmasammita is explained by the commentator either as having the same number of syllables in the names of the different Samans, or as equal to the Highest Self.
2 There are twelve months, five seasons, three worlds, then follows the sun as the twenty-first. Comm.
3 After having explained the secret meaning of the whole Sama- II PRAPA77/AKA, 13 KHANDA, 2. 29 breath. That is the Gayatra Saman, as interwoven in the (five) pra^as 1.

2. He who thus knows this Gayatra interwoven in the pra//as, keeps his senses, reaches the full life, he lives long2, becomes great with children and cattle, great by fame. The rule of him who thus meditates on the Gayatra is, ' Be not high-minded.'
Twelfth Khaa^da.

1. The hiiikara is, he rubs (the fire-stick); the prastava, smoke rises ; the udgitha, it burns ; the pratihara, there are glowing coals ; the nidhana, it goes down ; the nidhana, it is gone out. This is the Rathantara Saman as interwoven in fire 3.

2. He who thus knows this Rathantara interwoven in fire, becomes radiant4 and strong. He reaches the full life, he lives long, becomes great with children and cattle, great by fame. The rule is, ' Do not rinse the mouth or spit before the fire.'
Thirteenth Khaa^da.
1, 2. Next follows the Vamadevya as interwoven in generation 5.
zh: 第九‧堂
讓一個人以七重娑曼（Saman）冥想太陽。太陽是娑曼，因為它永遠是同一的（sama）；它是娑曼，因為它是同一的，每個人都認為它朝向我看，它朝向我看²。
讓他知道所有眾生都依賴於他（太陽）。他在升起之前的樣子，就是 *hiikara*。動物依賴於它。因此動物在日出之前說出 *hin*，因為牠們分享那個薩摩吠陀（*Saman*，太陽）的 *hiikara*。
3. 他初升之時的樣貌，即為序歌（prastava）。人們依賴於此。因此人們喜愛讚美（prastuti）和名聲，因為他們分享那薩曼讚歌（Saman）的序歌（prastava）。
這些是 stobhaksharas，即用於演唱薩摩吠陀讚歌時的音樂音節；見第 22 頁。
2 參考《罕得歐義書》(Kh. Up.) II, 2, 2。注疏。
二、《普拉帕托卡經》第九卷《克哈塔》第八篇27 4. 在沙摩瓦儀式（sarigava）之時他所是者，即是阿迪（adi）、最初者、唵（Om）。鳥類依靠於此。是故鳥類在天空中飛翔而無所寄託，自我支撐其身，蓋因牠們分有該沙摩讚（Saman）之阿迪（唵）。
5. 他正午時分之所是，即為鄔德基他（udgitha）。提婆依於它（因為他們光輝燦爛）。因此他們是梵天（Prajāpati）所有後裔中最優越者，蓋因他們分享該薩曼（Saman）之鄔德基他。
6. 他在正午之後及午後之前所是之物，即為pratihara（護門者）。一切胚種皆依存於此。因此這些胚種一旦經由孕育（pratihr'ta），便不致墮落，蓋因彼等分享了那梵讚歌（Saman）的pratihara。
他在午後至日落前的狀態，就是那個「烏帕德剌瓦」(upadrava)。林中的動物依賴於它。因此，當牠們看見人類時，牠們便逃往森林作為安全的藏身之處，因為牠們分享了那首頌歌的烏帕德剌瓦。
8. 太陽初設時的樣態，就是尼達那（nidhana）。眾父依賴於它。因此他們將其置下（nidadhati），因為他們分享那薩曼（Saman）的尼達那。如此，人沉思於七重薩曼如日輪。
當太陽放射其光芒，當母牛與其幼犢相聚之時，亦即如拉傑恩德拉拉爾‧米特拉（Rajendralal Mitra）所言，在母牛被擠奶之後，牧牛人允許其哺育幼畜之時。
第三項比較基礎在此乃 adi 的 a 與 adaya 的 a，即「持有」(holding)。d 本可被加入其中。
祖靈的供餅，或祖靈本身。
28 ## 歌者奧義書
第十經段第一章　其次，人應當冥想那自身統一且超越死亡之七重薩曼（Saman）。詞語「嗨伊卡拉」（hiiikara）有三個音節，詞語「普拉斯塔瓦」（prastava）有三個音節：這就是相等的（sama）。
二、詞語 adi（首先、唵 Om）有兩個音節，詞語 pratihara 有四個音節。從其中取出一個音節，那就相等（sama）了。
3. 「烏德吉塔」（udgitha）一詞有三個音節，「烏帕德拉瓦」（upadrava）一詞有四個音節。以三個加三個音節應當相等。剩下一個音節，它遂成為三音節。因此它是相等的。
4. 「nidhana」一詞具有三個音節，因此它是相等的。這些構成二十二個音節。
5. 以二十一個音節，一個人到達太陽（及死亡），因為太陽是從此處起的第二十一個；以第二十二個音節，他征服了太陽之外的事物：那就是福樂，那就是無憂無慮的境界。
他在此獲得對太陽（死亡）的勝利，而對於知道此事、靜觀七重薩曼（Saman）者而言，有一個更高於對太陽勝利之勝利——此薩曼在自身中是一致的，它引導超越死亡，是的，它引導超越死亡。
第十一奎奴達 3. 1. 嗚音（hi-ikara）是心智，前奏（prastava）是言語，唱詞（udgitha）是視覺，副歌（pratihara）是聽覺，終曲（nidhana）1 是自我相合（Atmasammita），注疏家將其解釋為在不同薩摩讚歌的名稱中具有相同音節數，或等同於最高的自我（Highest Self）。
有十二個月、五個季節、三個世界，然後太陽作為第二十一者隨之而來。注疏。
在說明整部《薩摩吠陀》(Sama-Veda)的深層含義之後——《往世書》(Purâna)第十三篇第二章載——氣息(prâṇa)。這就是《蓋亞特里頌歌》(Gayatra Saman)，如同織入五種生命氣(pra^as)之中。
2. 凡如此認識織入生命力（pra//as）之中的迦亞特里（Gayatra）者，能制伏其諸感官，達致圓滿之生命，其壽永長，以子孫牲畜而偉大，以名聲而偉大。如此冥想迦亞特里者的法則乃是：「勿傲慢自得。」
第十二篇《迦奧義》
希伊卡拉（hiiikara）是他摩擦（火棒）；普拉斯塔瓦（prastava）是煙升起；烏德吉塔（udgitha）是它燃燒；普拉提哈拉（pratihara）是有炭火發光；尼達納（nidhana）是它熄滅；尼達納（nidhana）是它熄滅了。這就是編織於火中的拉坦塔拉頌歌（Rathantara Saman）³。
2. 誰如此認知這首融合於火中的盧陀羅火讚歌（Rathantara），便成為光輝而強大者。他達致圓滿的生命，長壽久居，因子嗣與牲畜而偉大，因名聲而尊榮。其規則為：「在火前勿漱口或吐痰。」
第十三頌讚（Khaa^da）
1、2. 其次是《婆摩提婆頌》（Vamadevya），交織於第五代中。
